title: Breaking ground on India’s quantum future | IBM Quantum Computing Blog
why:
- Construction has begun on Quantum Valley Tech Park in Amaravati, which will be the home of India’s first IBM quantum computer.
- The tech park members already have access to IBM quantum computers over the cloud through a partnership between IBM and Tata Consultancy Services (TCS).
- A free online quantum computing course co-created by IBM has surpassed 208,785 enrollments for 2026, with over 100,000 participants from Andhra Pradesh.
- The Government of India’s NITI Aayog has reported the need to train approximately 100,000 quantum developers to ensure India’s leadership in quantum computing in the 2030s.
- IBM has helped integrate quantum education into India’s formal academic system by supporting new degree programs and training over 9,500 faculty members in the past year.
- The Quantum Valley Tech Park will host India’s first IBM Quantum System Two, providing scalable infrastructure to support research and workforce development.
